sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: US Army report confirms 374Water's AirSCWO tech destroys 99.9993% of PFAS in firefighting foam
feed.summary: A US Army Corps of Engineers report has independently validated 374Water's AirSCWO technology, confirming its ability to destroy 99.9993% of PFAS chemicals found in aqueous film-forming foam (AFFF). This demonstration, conducted with Arcadis, showed the system meets strict EPA standards with near-zero harmful emissions. The validation strengthens 374Water's readiness for deployment and its plans to expand into federal and commercial PFAS remediation markets, particularly for military sites with legacy contamination.

feed.title: D-Wave Quantum to Report Second Quarter 2026 Financial Results on August 6
feed.summary: D-Wave Quantum will release its second quarter 2026 financial results on August 6, 2026, before U.S. markets open. The company will host an investor conference call featuring CEO Dr. Alan Baratz and CFO John Markovich to discuss results and business outlook. The earnings release, webcast, and conference call materials will be available on D-Wave's investor relations website.

feed.title: Massachusetts EV Drivers Could Earn Incentives for Sending Power to Grid
feed.summary: Eversource and National Grid have partnered with EnergyHub, Sunrun, and The Mobility House to test a residential vehicle-to-grid (V2G) program in Massachusetts. This initiative will allow EV drivers to enroll their V2G-capable vehicles in the existing Connected Solutions program, enabling them to send stored energy back to the grid during peak demand and earn incentives while supporting grid reliability and affordability. The program aims to leverage the growing number of EVs as mobile power banks to reduce grid strain and lower costs for all customers.

feed.title: Sabre management to discuss second-quarter results and forward outlook Aug. 6
feed.summary: Sabre (NASDAQ: SABR) announced it will host a live webcast of its second quarter 2026 earnings conference call on August 6, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. ET. Management will review financial results and provide commentary on the forward outlook, with the webcast accessible via the Investor Relations section of investors.sabre.com. A replay will be available for at least 90 days following the event.

feed.title: Carrier acquires building automation firm 75F
feed.summary: Carrier Global Corporation announced the acquisition of 75F, a building automation systems company specializing in cloud-native, wireless, and AI-enabled technology. This acquisition enhances Carrier's portfolio with 75F’s AI and auto-commissioning capabilities, aiming to create more autonomous and self-optimizing buildings. Deepinder Singh, founder of 75F, will continue as CEO of the acquired business.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (NVR) NVR, Inc. Reports Q2 Revenue $2.28B, vs. FactSet Est of $2.40B
feed.summary: NVR, Inc. reported its Q2 revenue at $2.28 billion, falling short of FactSet's estimate of $2.40 billion. The company, a leading U.S. homebuilder, has recently been added to several Russell Growth benchmarks and authorized a $750 million share buyback plan. Analysts currently maintain a "HOLD" consensus with an average target price of $7,052.00.

feed.title: NVR Booked 9% More Home Orders as Q2 Settlements Fell 8%
feed.summary: NVR (NYSE: NVR) announced a decline in second-quarter 2026 net income and diluted EPS by 29% and 23% respectively, with consolidated revenues falling by 10% year-over-year. Despite a 9% increase in new home orders, settlements decreased by 8%, and the homebuilding gross margin narrowed due to higher lot costs and affordability pressures. The company also reported a decrease in mortgage banking closed loan production and a significant share repurchase program.

feed.title: Oceania Cruises Pairs Bangkok Tai Chi With Santorini Yoga
feed.summary: Oceania Cruises is expanding its wellness offerings with over 50 "Wellness Discovery Tours" across Asia, Europe, and South America, integrating local traditions like tai chi, dance therapy, and yoga. These small-group shore excursions are designed to complement onboard enrichment activities, providing guests with cultural immersion and personal well-being experiences. The initiative aims to cater to luxury travelers seeking transformative journeys that combine exploration with health and mindfulness.

feed.title: Bank of America extends naming rights deal for Panthers stadium amid $1.3B renovation
feed.summary: Bank of America has extended its naming rights deal for the Carolina Panthers' stadium, which is currently undergoing a significant $1.3 billion renovation. This agreement ensures the continued branding of the venue, which hosted 47 events last year, a substantial increase from 15 in 2018. The extended partnership aligns with the ongoing investment in the stadium's upgrade and its growing event calendar.

feed.title: Cogeco mobile customers get financing, protection and trade-ins for devices
feed.summary: Assurant and Cogeco Communications Inc. have partnered to offer Canadian mobile customers a comprehensive device solution, including access to network-compatible devices, flexible financing, protection, and trade-in options. This collaboration aims to make mobile ownership more affordable, flexible, and sustainable, addressing the growing demand for convenience and value in the mobile device lifecycle. The solution allows customers to browse and purchase devices, including new, refurbished, and certified Open Box models, directly through BestBuy.ca, with integrated device lifecycle management from Assurant.

feed.title: Sagtec launches Sage AI after securing a $1.1M deployment
feed.summary: Sagtec Global's CEO, Ng Chen Lok, outlined strategic and financial progress for the first half of 2026, building on a record FY2025 with $19.1 million revenue. The company launched its Sage AI enterprise platform, securing a $1.1 million deployment, and introduced the Halo AI POS System with a target of 10,000 subscribers by 2027. Sagtec is also evaluating a potential spin-off of its subsidiary, CL Technologies, to enhance strategic flexibility and capital access.

feed.title: Target Beats Tobacco Fee Suit as Judge Rebuffs Agency Guidance
feed.summary: A federal judge ruled in favor of Target Corp., upholding its practice of charging annual health care penalties to tobacco-using employees. The judge declined to defer to US Labor Department guidance, stating that Target's $800 annual penalty for smokers is an "all-or-nothing" proposition where workers can avoid the entire fee if they qualify for an exemption. This decision dismisses a lawsuit that accused Target of not reimbursing penalties for workers who later quit smoking.

feed.title: BlackRock, Coinbase, Strategy in group pledging $15 million to prepare Bitcoin for quantum threats
feed.summary: A consortium of nine companies, including BlackRock, Coinbase, and Strategy, has pledged $15 million over three years to fund Bitcoin security research and open-source development, specifically focusing on preparing for quantum computing threats. Each member will independently direct their funding, emphasizing that the group will not intervene in Bitcoin's governance or protocol decisions. This initiative aims to support post-quantum research as developers work on protections against future advances in quantum computing, which could potentially break current Bitcoin cryptography.

feed.title: NYSE Content Update: Fourth Annual AI x Bio Summit to Commence at the NYSE – Company Announcement
feed.summary: The New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) announced its pre-market update for July 23, 2026, highlighting the Fourth Annual AI x Bio Summit. Key participants will include Arkaea Media Group CEO Mo Islam, Decoding Bio Co-Founder Amee Kapadia, and Similarweb's Scott Trabucco, discussing market insights and data analysis. The update also noted market reactions to escalating tensions in the Middle East, with ICE Brent Crude Oil trading at $99 a barrel.

feed.title: Oppenheimer Adjusts Corteva Price Target to $95 From $89, Maintains Outperform Rating
feed.summary: Oppenheimer has increased its price target for Corteva (CTVA) to $95 from $89, reiterating an Outperform rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects analyst confidence in the agricultural chemicals company. Other recent analyst actions also show upward revisions to Corteva's price targets.

feed.title: Honeywell International Inc. Reports Earnings Results for the Second Quarter and Six Months Ended June 30, 2026
feed.summary: Honeywell International Inc. reported strong financial results for the second quarter and six months ended June 30, 2026. For Q2 2026, revenue increased to USD 9,719 million from USD 9,322 million year-over-year, and net income significantly rose to USD 5,682 million from USD 1,570 million. The company's diluted EPS from continuing operations also saw a substantial increase for both the quarter and the six-month period.

feed.title: Honeywell International (HON) Q2 2026 earnings summary
feed.summary: Honeywell International (HON) announced strong Q2 2026 results and raised its full-year guidance, driven by growth in Process Automation & Technologies and Industrial Automation. The company launched new three-year financial targets, emphasizing organic growth, margin expansion, and recurring software revenue. Honeywell also completed the acquisition of Johnson Matthey's Catalyst Technologies business and advanced its portfolio transformation initiatives.

feed.title: Thermo Fisher Scientific (TMO) Q2 2026 earnings summary
feed.summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific reported robust Q2 2026 results with a 10% year-over-year revenue increase to $11.99 billion and a 5% organic revenue growth. Diluted GAAP EPS rose by 9% to $4.68, and adjusted EPS increased by 13% to $6.03. The company saw strengthened customer activity, continued market share gains, and launched innovative products while updating its 2026 financial guidance.

feed.title: Top 3 Consumer Stocks You May Want To Dump This Month
feed.summary: This article identifies three consumer discretionary stocks that show signs of being overbought as of July 23, 2026, based on their Relative Strength Index (RSI) values. The stocks are Best Buy Co Inc (BBY), BJ’s Restaurants Inc (BJRI), and Wolverine World Wide Inc (WWW). Each company's recent stock performance, analyst actions, and RSI values are highlighted as indicators for investors considering momentum in their trading decisions.

feed.title: Franklin Resources (NYSE: BEN) sets $15M retention plan, carry pay
feed.summary: Franklin Resources Inc. has approved a one-time special retention compensation plan for its senior leadership, effective July 21, 2026. This includes equity awards with a grant date fair value of approximately $15 million for four executives, split between performance stock units (PSUs) and time-based restricted stock units (RSUs). Additionally, the CEO and Executive Chairman received carried interest incentives tied to future distributions from select private markets and alternative strategy funds, vesting over five years and subject to fund performance and potential clawbacks.

feed.title: Ameriprise Financial (AMP) Q2 2026 earnings summary
feed.summary: Ameriprise Financial (AMP) reported a strong Q2 2026 with adjusted operating EPS up 22% to $11.07 and total assets reaching $1.8 trillion, a 14% increase. The company's adjusted operating net revenues grew 13% to $4.9 billion, and it returned $932 million to shareholders, repurchasing 1.7 million shares. Ameriprise was recognized by Fortune and TIME for its innovation and strong performance, projecting sustained profitable growth and a robust balance sheet.

feed.title: Wireless sensors and AI join Carrier's push for autonomous buildings
feed.summary: Carrier (NYSE: CARR) has acquired 75F, a provider of cloud-native, wireless, AI-enabled building automation systems, to enhance its intelligent building strategy. This acquisition integrates 75F's advanced AI and auto-commissioning capabilities with Carrier's existing platforms, aiming to create more autonomous and self-optimizing buildings. The combined technologies are expected to improve deployment speed, energy optimization, and real-time thermal performance, particularly for data centers.

feed.title: UnitedHealth Just Beat Estimates by 30%. Wall Street Wants More.
feed.summary: UnitedHealth Group (UNH) surpassed Q2 adjusted earnings estimates by 30% and raised its full-year EPS guidance, primarily due to better-than-expected medical cost ratios and strong performance from its Optum unit. Despite this beat, Wall Street analysts have a modest mean target of $475, a 10% increase from the current price, while TIKR's model projects a 68% return by 2030, valuing the stock at $723. The company faces ongoing challenges with commercial insurance cost trends due to arbitration linked to the No Surprises Act.

feed.title: Truist Financial (NYSE: TFC) sells $1.25B 4.957% notes due 2030
feed.summary: Truist Financial Corporation announced the issuance and sale of $1.25 billion in 4.957% Fixed-to-Floating Rate Medium-Term Notes, Series I (Senior), due July 23, 2030. This debt offering adds a new financial obligation to the company, registered under the Securities Act of 1933 via a Form S-3 statement. The notes were issued on July 23, 2026, and a legal opinion regarding their validity was provided by Mayer Brown LLP.

feed.title: (QTOC) Movement as an Input in Quant Signal Sets
feed.summary: This article provides an AI-generated analysis for Innovator Growth Accelerated Plus Etf (NASDAQ: QTOC), highlighting neutral near and mid-term readings but a positive long-term bias. It outlines distinct institutional trading strategies, including position, momentum breakout, and risk hedging, alongside multi-timeframe signal analysis with support and resistance levels. The report emphasizes an exceptional 11.3:1 risk-reward short setup, signaling a potential 3.2% downside against a minimal 0.3% risk.

feed.title: Cigna AI tools to identify chronic condition patients, save $200M
feed.summary: Cigna Healthcare is expanding its AI-enabled predictive tools to identify customers with chronic or complex health conditions earlier, aiming to reduce medical costs by $200 million over the next three years. This initiative will extend clinical outreach to 20% more customers and is expected to save engaged customers an average of $2,000 per year in medical costs. Over 1,250 clinicians will use AI-generated insights to provide personalized support and prevent conditions from escalating.

feed.title: Applied Materials (AMAT) Stock Looks Reasonable Despite Its 321% Five Year Run
feed.summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) has shown a significant 321.1% return over the last five years, with a 198.4% return in the past year, while its valuation presents a mixed picture of being undervalued on some metrics and expensive on others. The stock trades at a P/E multiple of 51.7x, which is below the semiconductor industry average and Simply Wall St's tailored fair P/E of 58.9x, suggesting it may be undervalued based on earnings. The article concludes that the debate for investors centers on the durability of AI-related equipment demand and its ability to justify current pricing amidst geopolitical and cyclical risks.

feed.title: Dominion Energy faces pushback in northern Virginia over proposed transmission lines
feed.summary: Dominion Energy is facing significant pushback from communities in Nokesville and Bristow, Prince William County, Virginia, over proposed high-power transmission lines. Residents are concerned about the project, which aims to connect the Nokesville substation to a new Bristow switching station with 6.5 miles of 240-kilovolt lines. A community meeting is scheduled for Thursday at Patriot High School where residents are expected to advocate for an underground option.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs Reaffirms Their Buy Rating on Synchrony Financial (SYF)
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs has reiterated its Buy rating on Synchrony Financial (SYF) with a price target of $87.00. This follows similar Buy ratings from other analysts like Barclays, although BTIG maintained a Hold rating. Synchrony Financial reported strong financial results for the quarter ending March 31, with a revenue of $5.6 billion and a net

feed.title: 374Water says US Army report validates AirSCWO technology for PFAS destruction
feed.summary: 374Water Inc (NASDAQ:SCWO) announced that a US Army Corps of Engineers report validated its AirSCWO technology, achieving a 99.9993% destruction and removal efficiency for PFAS in aqueous film-forming foam (AFFF). The independent demonstration, conducted with Arcadis, confirmed the system's ability to destroy PFAS, meet US EPA effluent standards, and produce near-zero fluorinated emissions. This validation is expected to boost the technology's deployment in federal PFAS remediation markets and supports a memorandum of understanding with Arcadis for joint projects.

feed.title: Stephens raises RBB Bancorp stock price target to $29 on earnings beat
feed.summary: Stephens has increased its price target for RBB Bancorp (NASDAQ: RBB) to $29 from $26, maintaining an Equal Weight rating, following the bank's strong second-quarter earnings. RBB Bancorp reported operating EPS of $0.60, surpassing both Stephens' estimate and the Street's consensus, and the stock is considered undervalued with a low PEG ratio. The bank also announced a 1 million share buyback program and expects mid-to-high single-digit net loan growth for the second half of 2026.

feed.title: Qualcomm’s AI Story Is Hot. Why Is First Solar’s Future Clearer?
feed.summary: This article compares Qualcomm (QCOM) and First Solar (FSLR) as semiconductor exposure options, arguing that First Solar presents a more compelling future due to its locked-in demand and superior financial metrics. While Qualcomm has an exciting AI narrative, First Solar boasts clearer guidance, a massive contracted order book, a stronger technological and policy-backed moat, and better growth and profitability figures at a lower valuation. The author suggests First Solar offers proven results, whereas Qualcomm represents a more uncertain turnaround bet.

feed.title: Analysts Are Bullish on These Industrial Goods Stocks: Equifax (EFX), Rocket Lab USA (RKLB)
feed.summary: Stifel Nicolaus analysts have issued bullish ratings for Equifax (EFX) and Rocket Lab USA (RKLB). Shlomo Rosenbaum maintained a Buy rating on Equifax with a $235.00 price target, while Erik Rasmussen maintained a Buy rating on Rocket Lab USA with a $132.00 price target. Both companies also have a Strong Buy consensus rating from analysts, with significant upside potentials from current levels.

feed.title: Jensen Huang Turns to Japan’s Robots for Nvidia’s Next Growth Engine
feed.summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang is focusing on Japan's robotics and manufacturing sector as the next major growth area for the company, following its significant success in generative A.I. During a recent visit to Tokyo, Huang promoted "physical A.I.," emphasizing Japan's strong potential to combine its precision manufacturing with Nvidia's A.I. chips and software to lead this new industrial revolution. This initiative includes a partnership with a Japanese A.I. consortium to build a national physical A.I. infrastructure, aiming to leverage A.I. to address Japan's labor shortages and secure a dominant position in the global A.I.-robotics market.

feed.title: Roper Technologies raises annual profit forecast on AI software demand
feed.summary: Roper Technologies has increased its full-year adjusted profit forecast and revenue growth outlook due to strong demand for its AI-integrated software products. The company's CEO, Neil Hunn, highlighted the launch of new AI-enabled products, which expanded its addressable markets. Roper also reported second-quarter revenue and adjusted EPS that exceeded estimates, while also expecting a strong third quarter.

feed.title: Global Payments Schedules 8 a.m. Webcast for Q2 Results
feed.summary: Global Payments Inc. (NYSE: GPN) is scheduled to release its second-quarter financial results before the market opens on Wednesday, August 5, 2026. The company will also host a live audio webcast at 8:00 a.m. ET to discuss these results. Interested parties can access the webcast and its archived replay via the investor relations page on Global Payments' website.

feed.title: FDA clears Johnson & Johnson’s surgical robot for ‘the next era in surgery’
feed.summary: The FDA has approved Johnson & Johnson's Ottava Robotic Surgical System for several soft tissue surgeries, enabling J&J to compete with established players like Intuitive Surgical and Medtronic. J&J plans a limited commercial launch in the U.S. initially, focusing on customer success before expanding into more indications and regulatory jurisdictions. Despite Ottava's unique, space-saving design, analysts note that Intuitive Surgical's da Vinci robot remains the preferred choice due to its strong physician relationships and integration into hospital workflows.

feed.title: Tractor Supply misses estimates, cuts guidance on weak demand
feed.summary: Tractor Supply Company reported second-quarter results that fell short of analyst expectations and subsequently lowered its full-year outlook. The rural lifestyle retailer cited unusually adverse conditions in May, especially softness in seasonal and discretionary categories, as the primary reason for missing its sales targets. Consequently, the company updated its fiscal 2026 guidance, projecting lower adjusted EPS and net sales growth.

feed.title: Freeport-McMoran earnings beat by $0.15, revenue topped estimates
feed.summary: Freeport-McMoran (NYSE: FCX) announced second-quarter earnings per share of $0.74, surpassing analyst estimates by $0.15, and revenue of $7.03 billion, exceeding the consensus estimate of $6.71 billion. The company's stock has seen a 6.47% increase in the last three months and a 45.81% rise over the past year, indicating "good performance" according to InvestingPro.

feed.title: Norfolk Southern earnings beat by $0.29, revenue topped estimates
feed.summary: Norfolk Southern (NYSE: NSC) reported strong second-quarter results, exceeding analyst expectations with an

feed.title: Alphabet Smashes Earnings, Capex Sinks Stock. Too Much Too Soon?
feed.summary: Alphabet reported strong Q2 earnings, with revenue surpassing expectations, accelerated cloud growth, and significant profit surges, primarily due to investment portfolio gains. Despite the impressive financial performance, the stock fell 4% after hours because the company again raised its capital expenditure guidance for AI infrastructure, signaling increased spending. This highlights Wall Street's focus on future spending commitments over current profits in the competitive AI landscape.

feed.title: West Pharmaceutical Services (NYSE:WST) Beats Expectations in Strong Q2 CY2026, Stock Soars
feed.summary: West Pharmaceutical Services (NYSE:WST) reported strong Q2 CY2026 results, exceeding revenue and adjusted EPS expectations. The company saw a 13.8% year-on-year revenue increase to $872.3 million and an adjusted EPS of $2.37, beating analyst estimates by 13.9%. Following the positive report, the company also raised its full-year revenue and adjusted EPS guidance, causing its stock to rise by 7.3%.

feed.title: Skyworks launches network synchronizers for AI data centers By Investing.com
feed.summary: Skyworks Solutions Inc. has introduced its new SKY6911x/2x NetSync network synchronizers designed for AI data centers and high-speed communications infrastructure. These devices integrate advanced synchronization features, including IEEE 1588 Precision Time Protocol, and support high-speed SerDes interfaces. The launch aims to reduce system complexity and accelerate development for modern network infrastructure requiring precise timing.

feed.title: KeyCorp Raises Block (NYSE:XYZ) Price Target to $105.00
feed.summary: KeyCorp raised its price target for Block (NYSE:XYZ) to $105.00, maintaining an "overweight" rating, which suggests a 35% upside. This adjustment follows Block's strong Q2 earnings report, where the company surpassed EPS and revenue estimates and provided positive FY2026 guidance. While analyst sentiment is generally positive with a "Moderate Buy" consensus, insider selling activity has been noted over the last 90 days.

feed.title: Norfolk Southern: Q2 Earnings Snapshot
feed.summary: The article provides a Q2 earnings snapshot for Norfolk Southern. No specific financial details or further information are available in the provided content.

feed.title: Earnings Flash (NSC) Norfolk Southern Corporation Posts Q2 Adjusted EPS $3.52 per Share, vs. FactSet Est of $3.31
feed.summary: Norfolk Southern Corporation (NSC) reported strong second-quarter results, with adjusted EPS of $3.52 per share, exceeding FactSet's estimate of $3.31. The company's revenue also rose, reaching $3.5 billion, driven by increased freight demand and fuel surcharges. This positive performance was in line with other railroad operators like Union Pacific, which also posted higher quarterly profits.

feed.title: U.S. Bancorp (USB) Q2 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
feed.summary: U.S. Bancorp reported strong Q2 2026 earnings with diluted EPS up 22% year-over-year and total net revenue growing 10.1%, driven by higher net interest income and significant fee momentum. The company achieved 400 basis points of positive operating leverage, improved its efficiency ratio, and successfully integrated the BTIG acquisition, which contributed $98 million in its first month. USB also raised its full-year 2026 revenue guidance to 7%-9% growth, reflecting robust loan demand, continued fee income expansion, and the anticipated acquisition of a $1.6 billion Amazon small business portfolio.

feed.title: BofA Securities Adjusts United Rentals Price Target to $1,300 From $1,195
feed.summary: BofA Securities has increased its price target for United Rentals (URI) to $1,300 from $1,195. This adjustment follows several other analysts revising their price targets for the company upwards in July 2026. The financial update underscores positive sentiment around United Rentals' stock.

feed.title: Rli Releases Q2 2026 Financial Results
feed.summary: RLI Corp. reported strong second-quarter 2026 financial results, with operating earnings of $0.83 per share, surpassing analyst expectations. The company achieved significant revenue growth of 15.2% year-over-year, reaching $575.6 million, driven by robust performance in its Casualty segment. RLI maintained a strong combined ratio of 85.6%, reflecting disciplined underwriting in specialty insurance markets.

feed.title: Why is Huntington Bancshares stock sliding today? By Investing.com
feed.summary: Huntington Bancshares (HBAN) stock fell 3.4% in pre-open trading after its Q2 2026 earnings missed Wall Street expectations, particularly on earnings per share. This disappointment, coupled with a recent downgrade by Jefferies, negative insider selling activity, and a challenging economic environment, contributed to the selling pressure. Despite some analysts maintaining "buy" ratings, the weak Q2 results failed to alleviate concerns about the bank's margin trajectory and integration execution.
